text: Patient transferred from hospital to skilled nursing facility for rehabilitation to build strength before returning home
transcript_quote: they've transferred you here for skilled nursing care — to build your strength back up before you go home

text: Patient reports generalized fatigue, everything wears her out
transcript_quote: Everything wears me out these days.

text: Type 2 diabetes mellitus, sugars running higher than desired
transcript_quote: You have type two diabetes

text: Stage 1 chronic kidney disease attributed to diabetes
transcript_quote: it has started to touch your kidneys — stage one chronic kidney disease, the earliest stage

text: Hypertension (blood pressure runs high)
transcript_quote: Your blood pressure runs high.

text: Anemia, treated with vitamin B12 injections
transcript_quote: You have anemia, and you receive vitamin B12 injections for it.

text: Hypertriglyceridemia / metabolic syndrome (high triglycerides)
transcript_quote: Your triglycerides run high as well, part of what we call metabolic syndrome.

text: Patient reports polydipsia — constant thirst, never satisfied
transcript_quote: I'm thirsty all the time, for one. I keep a glass by my chair and a bottle by the bed and it's never enough.

text: Patient reports nocturia, urinating 4-5 times per night
transcript_quote: I'm up to the bathroom all night long. Four, five times a night.

text: Patient reports polyphagia — increased hunger and eating despite feeling weak/emptied out
transcript_quote: I'm hungry too. I've been eating more than I ever did, and still I feel emptied out. Weak.

text: Patient reports bilateral tingling/paresthesias in feet and hands, worse at night, developed over months
transcript_quote: They tingle. Both of them, and my hands some too. Pins and needles, like they fell asleep and won't wake all the way up. It's worst at night when I'm trying to rest.

text: Patient reports increased daytime napping/sleeping half the day (reported by daughter)
transcript_quote: She was also napping half the day, which is not my mother.

text: Patient reports mild cognitive fogginess and word-finding difficulty (intermittent, recovers)
transcript_quote: I've been losing my word for things lately. It comes back, but it takes the scenic route.

text: Clinician attributes hyperglycemic symptoms (thirst, hunger, polyuria, paresthesias) to poorly controlled diabetes
transcript_quote: those symptoms — the thirst, the hunger, all the trips to the bathroom, the tingling in the hands and feet — those point straight back at the diabetes, and they tell me your sugars have been running higher than we want

text: Clopidogrel 75 mg orally once daily (antiplatelet)
transcript_quote: Clopidogrel, seventy-five milligrams once a day — that's a blood thinner.

text: Simvastatin 20 mg orally at night
transcript_quote: Simvastatin, twenty milligrams at night, for the blood fats.

text: Metoprolol extended-release 100 mg for blood pressure and heart rate
transcript_quote: The long-acting metoprolol, one hundred milligrams, for blood pressure and heart rate.

text: Vitamin B12 injection on a schedule for anemia
transcript_quote: The vitamin B12 injection on a schedule for the anemia.

text: Nitroglycerin sublingual spray PRN for chest tightness
transcript_quote: a nitroglycerin spray you keep with you, for under the tongue if you ever feel chest tightness

text: Patient denies recent chest tightness / has not needed nitroglycerin in a long while
transcript_quote: I haven't needed it in a good long while, thank the Lord.

text: Counseled patient to press call button and report immediately if chest pressure occurs rather than self-treating
transcript_quote: if you ever feel that chest pressure while you're here, you press the call button first. Don't just quietly reach into the handbag.

text: Allergy: aspirin, causes swelling
transcript_quote: Aspirin swells me up.

text: Allergy: peanuts, severe — throat itching even with peanut dust
transcript_quote: Peanuts are the dangerous one — even peanut dust starts my throat itching.

text: Allergy: animal dander — cannot tolerate cats or dogs
transcript_quote: I can't be around cats or dogs for long

text: Ordered peanut-free flag on all meal trays and diabetic-friendly diet accommodations
transcript_quote: The kitchen gets a peanut-free flag on every one of your trays

text: Therapy dog to be kept off patient's hallway due to animal dander allergy
transcript_quote: we have a therapy dog that makes rounds on Thursdays, so we'll keep him off your hallway

text: Aspirin allergy to be documented prominently on front of chart
transcript_quote: the aspirin allergy goes in large letters on the front of the chart

text: Patient reports poor oral intake, barely eating at hospital
transcript_quote: She barely touched anything at the hospital.

text: Dietitian referral for diabetic-friendly, peanut-free meal planning based on patient preferences
transcript_quote: A dietitian is part of your team here. Meals will be diabetic-friendly and built around the peanut allergy

text: History and physical examination performed
transcript_quote: This morning I'll do a complete history and physical exam.

text: Initial patient assessment performed by nursing
transcript_quote: The nursing staff will do their own initial assessment.

text: Development of individualized plan of care by interdisciplinary team
transcript_quote: the whole team sits down together and writes what we call an individualized plan of care — that's the map for your entire stay

text: Physical therapy ordered several times weekly for walking, balance, and transfers
transcript_quote: Physical therapy will work with you several times a week — walking, balance, getting out of a chair without it becoming a production.

text: Occupational therapy ordered for activities of daily living (bathing, dressing, kitchen tasks)
transcript_quote: Occupational therapy comes too, and they handle the daily-living side — bathing safely, dressing, kitchen tasks.

text: Speech-language therapy ordered for swallowing safety and cognitive/memory assessment
transcript_quote: The speech therapist checks swallowing safety, and runs through thinking and memory exercises.

text: Nursing care/supplementary surveillance every shift — vital signs, blood sugars, medications, skin checks
transcript_quote: the nurses will check on you every shift, every day — vital signs, blood sugars, medicines, skin checks, the whole picture

text: Weekly interdisciplinary team review of care plan (nursing, therapy, dietitian, social services, physician)
transcript_quote: once a week, the full team — nursing, therapy, the dietitian, social services, and me — sits down and reviews how the plan is working

text: Pre-discharge assessment planned to evaluate ability to manage medications, meals, stairs, and home safety before discharge
transcript_quote: when you're getting close, we do a formal pre-discharge assessment. Can you manage your medicines, your meals, the stairs, is the house set up safely.

text: SDOH: patient lives alone; daughter lives 40 minutes away with her own family — discharge readiness for independent self-care is a priority
transcript_quote: I live alone, doctor. Denise is forty minutes away with a family of her own. If I go home, I need to be able to do for myself.

text: SDOH: patient reports difficulty cooking proper diabetic meals for one person and let self-care slide at home
transcript_quote: I let things slide at home. Cooking a proper diabetic meal for one person is a sorry business.

text: Ordered to add scheduled vitamin B12 injection to facility schedule so dose does not lapse
transcript_quote: The nurses will get the B12 onto the facility schedule today so nothing lapses.

text: Care regimes assessment performed
History and physical note documented
